metadata.name: Configure a Persistent System Journal
metadata.description: Configure a system journal to make the log events persist after a reboot
objectives: Configure a system journal to preserve the log events after a reboot

hosts.name: servera
hosts.description: Target server for persistent journal configuration

tasks.name: Verify /var/log/journal directory exists
tasks.description: The /var/log/journal directory must exist for persistent journal storage

tasks.name: Verify journal files exist in persistent storage
tasks.description: Journal files should exist in a subdirectory of /var/log/journal
tasks.success_message: Journal files exist in persistent storage
tasks.failure_message: No journal files found in persistent storage
hints: You may need to reboot for journals to persist

tasks.name: Verify system.journal file exists
tasks.description: The system.journal file should exist for system-wide logs
tasks.failure_message: The system.journal file does not exist
hints: Run: journalctl --flush to write journal to disk
The system.journal file stores system-wide logs

tasks.name: Verify persistent journal is readable
tasks.description: The persistent journal should be readable with journalctl
tasks.success_message: Persistent journal is readable and contains boot records
tasks.failure_message: Persistent journal is not working correctly
hints: Use: journalctl --list-boots to see available boot logs
If only one boot is listed, the journal became persistent after the last boot
Multiple boots indicate the journal persisted across reboots
